PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST)  \n31\t**Severity:** High\n32\t\n33\t**Issue Description:**\n34\tARMOR does not return the `x-amz-request-id` header in any responses (success or error), while AWS S3 returns this header for all responses.\n35\t\n36\t**Expected Behavior:**\n37\t```http\n38\tx-amz-request-id: 4442587FB7D0A2F9\n39\t```\n40\t\n41\t**Actual Behavior:**\n42\t```http\n43\t# Header absent\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Impact:**\n47\t- Breaks AWS SDK compatibility (SDKs expect this header)\n48\t- Makes request tracing impossible for debugging\n49\t- Prevents client-server log correlation\n50\t- Limits support troubleshooting capabilities\n51\t\n52\t**AWS S3 Reference:**\n53\t- Always present in AWS S3 responses\n54\t- Used for support ticket debugging\n55\t- Required for distributed system tracing\n56\t\n57\t**Remediation:**\n58\t```go\n59\t// Add middleware to generate UUID for each request\n60\tfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n61\t    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n62\t        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n63\t        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n64\t        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n65\t    })\n66\t}\n67\t```\n68\t\n69\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n70\t\n71\t---\n72\t\n73\t#### H2: Missing `RequestId` XML Element\n74\t\n75\t**Type:** Protocol Compliance `<RequestId>` element that AWS S3 includes in all error responses.\n82\t\n83\t**Expected Behavior:**\n84\t```xml\n85\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n86\t<Error>\n87\t  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n88\t  <Message>Object not found</Message>\n89\t  <RequestId>4442587FB7D0A2F9</RequestId>\n90\t</Error>\n91\t```\n92\t\n93\t**Actual Behavior:**\n94\t```xml\n95\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n96\t<Error>\n97\t  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n98\t  <Message>Object not found</Message>\n99\t</Error>\n100\t```\n101\t\n102\t**Impact:**\n103\t- AWS-compatible tools cannot parse request IDs\n104\t- Debugging frameworks cannot extract trace information\n105\t- Some S3-compatible libraries may fail to parse errors\n106\t\n107\t**Remediation:**\n108\tUpdate `writeError` functions to include RequestId:\n109\t```go\n110\tfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int, requestID string) {\n111\t    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n112\t    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n113\t    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n114\t<Error>\n115\t  <Code>%s</Code>\n116\t  <Message>%s</Message>\n117\t  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n118\t</Error>`, code, message, requestID)\n119\t}\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n123\t\n124\t---\n125\t\n126\t### Medium-High Severity (Should Fix)\n127\t\n128\t#### MH1: Missing `x-amz-id-2` Header\n129\t\n130\t**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n131\t**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n132\t**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 operations  \n133\t**Severity:** Medium-High\n134\t\n135\t**Issue Description:**\n136\tARMOR does not return the `x-amz-id-2` extended request ID header that AWS S3 includes.\n137\t\n138\t**Expected Behavior:**\n139\t```http\n140\tx-amz-id-2: abcdefghij\n141\t```\n142\t\n143\t**Actual Behavior:**\n144\t```http\n145\t# Header absent\n146\t```\n147\t\n148\t**Impact:**\n149\t- S3-specific debugging tools may not work correctly\n150\t- Extended request tracing unavailable\n151\t- Partial AWS S3 header compliance\n152\t\n153\t**Remediation:**\n154\t```go\n155\t// Generate extended ID (can be opaque string)\n156\tw.Header().Set(\"x-amz-id-2\", generateExtendedID())\n157\t```\n158\t\n159\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n160\t\n161\t---\n162\t\n163\t#### MH2: Content-Type Mismatch - JSON in Plain Text Wrapper\n164\t\n165\t**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n166\t**Component:** Admin endpoints  \n167\t**Affected Endpoints:** `/admin/b2/keys`, `/admin/b2/keys/{id}`  \n168\t**Severity:** Medium-High\n169\t\n170\t**Issue Description:**\n171\tB2 key management endpoints return JSON response bodies but declare `Content-Type: text/plain` in error cases.\n172\t\n173\t**Expected Behavior:**\n174\t```http\n175\tHTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\n176\tContent-Type: application/json\n177\t\n178\t{\"error\":\"Failed to list keys: backend unavailable\"}\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t**Actual Behavior:**\n182\t```http\n183\tHTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\n184\tContent-Type: text/plain\n185\t\n186\t{\"error\":\"Failed to list keys: backend unavailable\"}\n187\t```\n188\t\n189\t**Impact:**\n190\t- Clients must parse JSON despite incorrect content type\n191\t- Some clients may fail to parse response\n192\t- Inconsistent with HTTP content negotiation\n193\t\n194\t**Remediation:**\n195\tUpdate `internal/server/server.go` to set correct content type for B2 error responses.\n196\t\n197\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:1246-1364`\n198\t\n199\t**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n200\t\n201\t---\n202\t\n203\t### Medium Severity (Consider Fixing)\n204\t\n205\t#### M1: Missing `Resource` XML Element\n206\t\n207\t**Type:** Completeness  \n208\t**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n209\t**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 error responses  \n210\t**Severity:** Medium\n211\t\n212\t**Issue Description:**\n213\tARMOR's error XML responses omit the `<Resource>` element that identifies the affected bucket/key.\n214\t\n215\t**Expected Behavior:**\n216\t```xml\n217\t<Resource>/mybucket/mykey</Resource>\n218\t```\n219\t\n220\t**Actual Behavior:**\n221\t```xml\n222\t<!-- Resource element absent -->\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t**Impact:**\n226\t- Clients cannot programmatically extract affected resource\n227\t- Debugging context is limited\n228\t- Some S3-compatible tools may expect this element\n229\t\n230\t**Remediation:**\n231\tExtract resource path from request and include in error responses.\n232\t\n233\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n234\t\n235\t---\n236\t\n237\t#### M2: Mixed Response Formats Within Endpoints\n238\t\n239\t**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n240\t**Component:** Admin endpoints  \n241\t**Affected Endpoints:** `/admin/key/rotate`, `/admin/key/export`, `/admin/presign`, `/armor/canary`  \n242\t**Severity:** Medium\n243\t\n244\t**Issue Description:**\n245\tSeveral admin endpoints return JSON for success but plain text or XML for errors, creating format inconsistency.\n246\t\n247\t**Examples:**\n248\t\n249\t| Endpoint | Success Format | Error Format | Inconsistency |\n250\t|----------|---------------|--------------|---------------|\n251\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Mixed |\n252\t| `/admin/key/export` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Mixed |\n253\t| `/admin/presign` | JSON | XML (auth) / Plain text (other) | ⚠️ Multiple |\n254\t| `/armor/canary` | JSON | Plain text (405) | ⚠️ Mixed |\n255\t\n256\t**Impact:**\n257\t- Clients must handle multiple response formats\n258\t- Inconsistent API experience\n259\t- Harder to write generic error handlers\n260\t\n261\t**Remediation:**\n262\tStandardize all admin endpoint errors to use JSON format:\n263\t```json\n264\t{\n265\t  \"error\": \"error message\",\n266\t  \"code\": \"ErrorCode\"\n267\t}\n268\t```\n269\t\n270\t**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n271\t\n272\t---\n273\t\n274\t#### M3: Method Not Allowed Format Inconsistency\n275\t\n276\t**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n277\t**Component:** All endpoints  \n278\t**Affected Endpoints:** All endpoints (405 responses)  \n279\t**Severity:** Medium\n280\t\n281\t**Issue Description:**\n282\tAll 405 Method Not Allowed responses use Go's `http.Error()` which returns plain text, even when the endpoint normally returns JSON.\n283\t\n284\t**Pattern:**\n285\t```http\n286\tHTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed\n287\tContent-Type: text/plain\n288\t\n289\tMethod not allowed\n290\t```\n291\t\n292\t**Impact:**\n293\t- Inconsistent with endpoint's normal response format\n294\t- API clients expecting JSON receive plain text\n295\t- Cannot include additional metadata (allowed methods)\n296\t\n297\t**Remediation:**\n298\tReplace generic 405 responses with structured JSON:\n299\t```json\n300\t{\n301\t  \"error\": \"Method not allowed\",\n302\t  \"code\": \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n303\t  \"allowed_methods\": [\"GET\", \"POST\"]\n304\t}\n305\t```\n306\t\n307\t**Code Reference:** All endpoints use `http.Error()` for 405 responses\n308\t\n309\t**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n310\t\n311\t---\n312\t\n313\t#### M4: Duplicate `writeError` Implementations\n314\t\n315\t**Type:** Code Quality  \n316\t**Component:** S3-facing and Admin endpoints  \n317\t**Affected Files:** `internal/server/server.go`, `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go`  \n318\t**Severity:** Medium\n319\t\n320\t**Issue Description:**\n321\tIdentical `writeError` functions exist in two files, creating maintenance risk.\n322\t\n323\t**Impact:**\n324\t- Changes must be made in two places\n325\t- Risk of divergence over time\n326\t- Maintenance burden\n327\t\n328\t**Remediation:**\n329\tExtract to shared utility package:\n330\t```go\n331\t// internal/pkg/errors/errors.go\n332\tfunc WriteS3Error(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int)\n333\t```\n334\t\n335\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm, bf-a5evuz\n336\t\n337\t---\n338\t\n339\t### Low Severity (Optional)\n340\t\n341\t#### L1: CORS Headers Always Present\n342\t\n343\t**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n344\t**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n345\t**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 operations  \n346\t**Severity:** Low\n347\t\n348\t**Issue Description:**\n349\tARMOR always includes CORS headers on all responses (including errors), while AWS S3 only includes CORS headers when explicitly configured on the bucket.\n350\t\n351\t**ARMOR Behavior:**\n352\t```http\n353\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n354\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n355\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n356\t```\n357\t\n358\t**AWS S3 Behavior:**\n359\t- CORS headers only present when bucket has CORS configuration\n360\t- Headers reflect bucket-specific allowed origins/methods\n361\t- Not present on errors unless origin matches\n362\t\n363\t**Impact:**\n364\t- Behavior differs from AWS but does not break functionality\n365\t- CORS is always enabled (may be overly permissive)\n366\t- May confuse debugging when comparing to AWS\n367\t\n368\t**Remediation:**\n369\tMake CORS behavior configurable to match AWS bucket-based configuration.\n370\t\n371\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm, bf-60v3ao\n372\t\n373\t---\n374\t\n375\t#### L2: Missing `HostId` XML Element\n376\t\n377\t**Type:** Completeness  \n378\t**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n379\t**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 error responses  \n380\t**Severity:** Low\n381\t\n382\t**Issue Description:**\n383\tARMOR's error XML responses omit the `<HostId>` element (extended request ID for S3-specific debugging).\n384\t\n385\t**Impact:**\n386\t- Rarely used by external clients\n387\t- Primarily for AWS internal debugging\n388\t- Minimal real-world impact\n389\t\n390\t**Remediation:**\n391\tOptional - add for full AWS compatibility.\n392\t\n393\t**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n394\t\n395\t---\n396\t\n397\t## Part 2: Coverage Gaps\n398\t\n399\t### Scenarios Lacking Test Coverage\n400\t\n401\t#### Gap 1: Pre-signed URL Error Scenarios\n402\t\n403\t**Type:** Test Coverage  \n404\t**Component:** `/share/*` endpoints  \n405\t**Missing Coverage:**\n406\t- Expired token validation\n407\t- Invalid signature verification\n408\t- Edge cases in token parsing\n409\t- Concurrent access to same pre-signed URL\n410\t\n411\t**Impact:**\n412\t- Pre-signed URL error handling not verified\n413\t- Edge cases may cause unexpected behavior\n414\t\n415\t**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n416\t\n417\t---\n418\t\n419\t#### Gap 2: Backend Error Propagation\n420\t\n421\t**Type:** Test Coverage  \n422\t**Component:** All S3 operations  \n423\t**Missing Coverage:**\n424\t- B2 rate limiting responses\n425\t- B2 network timeout handling\n426\t- Storage full errors\n427\t- Authentication failures to backend\n428\t\n429\t**Impact:**\n430\t- Backend error scenarios not tested\n431\t- May not map correctly to S3 error codes\n432\t\n433\t**Remediation Priority:** High\n434\t\n435\t---\n436\t\n437\t#### Gap 3: Concurrent Request Handling\n438\t\n439\t**Type:** Test Coverage  \n440\t**Component:** Authentication layer  \n441\t**Missing Coverage:**\n442\t- Multiple simultaneous auth failures\n443\t- Race conditions in signature verification\n444\t- Concurrent access to rate-limited endpoints\n445\t\n446\t**Impact:**\n447\t- Concurrency bugs may not be caught\n448\t- Production load patterns not tested\n449\t\n450\t**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n451\t\n452\t---\n453\t\n454\t#### Gap 4: Header Ordering Tests\n455\t\n456\t**Type:** Test Coverage  \n457\t**Component:** All endpoints  \n458\t**Missing Coverage:**\n459\t- Tests that verify header order consistency\n460\t- Tests for header case sensitivity\n461\t- Tests for duplicate header detection\n462\t\n463\t**Impact:**\n464\t- Header ordering may be inconsistent (noted in auth rejection docs)\n465\t- Some clients may be sensitive to header order\n466\t\n467\t**Remediation Priority:** Low\n468\t\n469\t---\n470\t\n471\t#### Gap 5: Large Payload Error Handling\n472\t\n473\t**Type:** Test Coverage  \n474\t**Component:** PUT operations  \n475\t**Missing Coverage:**\n476\t- Error responses during large file uploads\n477\t- Multipart upload abortion scenarios\n478\t- Range request errors on large files\n479\t\n480\t**Impact:**\n481\t- Large file error scenarios not verified\n482\t- Memory issues may not be caught\n483\t\n484\t**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n485\t\n486\t---\n487\t\n488\t### Protocol Compliance Gaps\n489\t\n490\t#### Gap 6: Chunked Encoding Error Responses\n491\t\n492\t**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n493\t**Component:** Streaming operations  \n494\t**Missing Implementation:**\n495\t- Error responses during chunked upload\n496\t- Trailer header handling on errors\n497\t- Early termination signaling\n498\t\n499\t**Impact:**\n500\t- Streaming clients may not handle errors correctly\n501\t- May not fully support chunked encoding spec\n502\t\n503\t**Remediation Priority:** ARMOR Header Consistency Issues and Gaps Documentation\n\n**Version:** 1.0  \n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Related Bead:** bf-2oq6du  \n**Status:** Complete\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive catalog of all identified header inconsistencies, gaps, and deviations from expected behavior across ARMOR's S3-facing and admin endpoints. Issues are categorized by severity and type to prioritize remediation efforts.\n\n---\n\n## Part 1: Header Inconsistencies by Severity\n\n### Critical Severity (Blocking Issues)\n\n**Status:** ✅ None identified\n\nNo critical header inconsistencies that block core functionality were identified.\n\n---\n\n### High Severity (Must Fix)\n\n#### H1: Missing `x-amz-request-id` Header\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 operations (GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST)  \n**Severity:** High\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR does not return the `x-amz-request-id` header in any responses (success or error), while AWS S3 returns this header for all responses.\n\n**Expected Behavior:**\n```http\nx-amz-request-id: 4442587FB7D0A2F9\n```\n\n**Actual Behavior:**\n```http\n# Header absent\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- Breaks AWS SDK compatibility (SDKs expect this header)\n- Makes request tracing impossible for debugging\n- Prevents client-server log correlation\n- Limits support troubleshooting capabilities\n\n**AWS S3 Reference:**\n- Always present in AWS S3 responses\n- Used for support ticket debugging\n- Required for distributed system tracing\n\n**Remediation:**\n```go\n// Add middleware to generate UUID for each request\nfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n\n---\n\n#### H2: Missing `RequestId` XML Element\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 error responses  \n**Severity:** High\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR's error XML responses omit the `<RequestId>` element that AWS S3 includes in all error responses.\n\n**Expected Behavior:**\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>Object not found</Message>\n  <RequestId>4442587FB7D0A2F9</RequestId>\n</Error>\n```\n\n**Actual Behavior:**\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n  <Message>Object not found</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- AWS-compatible tools cannot parse request IDs\n- Debugging frameworks cannot extract trace information\n- Some S3-compatible libraries may fail to parse errors\n\n**Remediation:**\nUpdate `writeError` functions to include RequestId:\n```go\nfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int, requestID string) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n</Error>`, code, message, requestID)\n}\n```\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n\n---\n\n### Medium-High Severity (Should Fix)\n\n#### MH1: Missing `x-amz-id-2` Header\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 operations  \n**Severity:** Medium-High\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR does not return the `x-amz-id-2` extended request ID header that AWS S3 includes.\n\n**Expected Behavior:**\n```http\nx-amz-id-2: abcdefghij\n```\n\n**Actual Behavior:**\n```http\n# Header absent\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- S3-specific debugging tools may not work correctly\n- Extended request tracing unavailable\n- Partial AWS S3 header compliance\n\n**Remediation:**\n```go\n// Generate extended ID (can be opaque string)\nw.Header().Set(\"x-amz-id-2\", generateExtendedID())\n```\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n\n---\n\n#### MH2: Content-Type Mismatch - JSON in Plain Text Wrapper\n\n**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n**Component:** Admin endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** `/admin/b2/keys`, `/admin/b2/keys/{id}`  \n**Severity:** Medium-High\n\n**Issue Description:**\nB2 key management endpoints return JSON response bodies but declare `Content-Type: text/plain` in error cases.\n\n**Expected Behavior:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\"error\":\"Failed to list keys: backend unavailable\"}\n```\n\n**Actual Behavior:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n{\"error\":\"Failed to list keys: backend unavailable\"}\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- Clients must parse JSON despite incorrect content type\n- Some clients may fail to parse response\n- Inconsistent with HTTP content negotiation\n\n**Remediation:**\nUpdate `internal/server/server.go` to set correct content type for B2 error responses.\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:1246-1364`\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n\n---\n\n### Medium Severity (Consider Fixing)\n\n#### M1: Missing `Resource` XML Element\n\n**Type:** Completeness  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 error responses  \n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR's error XML responses omit the `<Resource>` element that identifies the affected bucket/key.\n\n**Expected Behavior:**\n```xml\n<Resource>/mybucket/mykey</Resource>\n```\n\n**Actual Behavior:**\n```xml\n<!-- Resource element absent -->\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- Clients cannot programmatically extract affected resource\n- Debugging context is limited\n- Some S3-compatible tools may expect this element\n\n**Remediation:**\nExtract resource path from request and include in error responses.\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n\n---\n\n#### M2: Mixed Response Formats Within Endpoints\n\n**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n**Component:** Admin endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** `/admin/key/rotate`, `/admin/key/export`, `/admin/presign`, `/armor/canary`  \n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Issue Description:**\nSeveral admin endpoints return JSON for success but plain text or XML for errors, creating format inconsistency.\n\n**Examples:**\n\n| Endpoint | Success Format | Error Format | Inconsistency |\n|----------|---------------|--------------|---------------|\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Mixed |\n| `/admin/key/export` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Mixed |\n| `/admin/presign` | JSON | XML (auth) / Plain text (other) | ⚠️ Multiple |\n| `/armor/canary` | JSON | Plain text (405) | ⚠️ Mixed |\n\n**Impact:**\n- Clients must handle multiple response formats\n- Inconsistent API experience\n- Harder to write generic error handlers\n\n**Remediation:**\nStandardize all admin endpoint errors to use JSON format:\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": \"error message\",\n  \"code\": \"ErrorCode\"\n}\n```\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n\n---\n\n#### M3: Method Not Allowed Format Inconsistency\n\n**Type:** Format Inconsistency  \n**Component:** All endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All endpoints (405 responses)  \n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Issue Description:**\nAll 405 Method Not Allowed responses use Go's `http.Error()` which returns plain text, even when the endpoint normally returns JSON.\n\n**Pattern:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Impact:**\n- Inconsistent with endpoint's normal response format\n- API clients expecting JSON receive plain text\n- Cannot include additional metadata (allowed methods)\n\n**Remediation:**\nReplace generic 405 responses with structured JSON:\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Method not allowed\",\n  \"code\": \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n  \"allowed_methods\": [\"GET\", \"POST\"]\n}\n```\n\n**Code Reference:** All endpoints use `http.Error()` for 405 responses\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-a5evuz\n\n---\n\n#### M4: Duplicate `writeError` Implementations\n\n**Type:** Code Quality  \n**Component:** S3-facing and Admin endpoints  \n**Affected Files:** `internal/server/server.go`, `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go`  \n**Severity:** Medium\n\n**Issue Description:**\nIdentical `writeError` functions exist in two files, creating maintenance risk.\n\n**Impact:**\n- Changes must be made in two places\n- Risk of divergence over time\n- Maintenance burden\n\n**Remediation:**\nExtract to shared utility package:\n```go\n// internal/pkg/errors/errors.go\nfunc WriteS3Error(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int)\n```\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm, bf-a5evuz\n\n---\n\n### Low Severity (Optional)\n\n#### L1: CORS Headers Always Present\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 operations  \n**Severity:** Low\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR always includes CORS headers on all responses (including errors), while AWS S3 only includes CORS headers when explicitly configured on the bucket.\n\n**ARMOR Behavior:**\n```http\nAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\nAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\nAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n```\n\n**AWS S3 Behavior:**\n- CORS headers only present when bucket has CORS configuration\n- Headers reflect bucket-specific allowed origins/methods\n- Not present on errors unless origin matches\n\n**Impact:**\n- Behavior differs from AWS but does not break functionality\n- CORS is always enabled (may be overly permissive)\n- May confuse debugging when comparing to AWS\n\n**Remediation:**\nMake CORS behavior configurable to match AWS bucket-based configuration.\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm, bf-60v3ao\n\n---\n\n#### L2: Missing `HostId` XML Element\n\n**Type:** Completeness  \n**Component:** S3-facing endpoints  \n**Affected Endpoints:** All S3 error responses  \n**Severity:** Low\n\n**Issue Description:**\nARMOR's error XML responses omit the `<HostId>` element (extended request ID for S3-specific debugging).\n\n**Impact:**\n- Rarely used by external clients\n- Primarily for AWS internal debugging\n- Minimal real-world impact\n\n**Remediation:**\nOptional - add for full AWS compatibility.\n\n**Related Beads:** bf-1kbuqm\n\n---\n\n## Part 2: Coverage Gaps\n\n### Scenarios Lacking Test Coverage\n\n#### Gap 1: Pre-signed URL Error Scenarios\n\n**Type:** Test Coverage  \n**Component:** `/share/*` endpoints  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- Expired token validation\n- Invalid signature verification\n- Edge cases in token parsing\n- Concurrent access to same pre-signed URL\n\n**Impact:**\n- Pre-signed URL error handling not verified\n- Edge cases may cause unexpected behavior\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n\n---\n\n#### Gap 2: Backend Error Propagation\n\n**Type:** Test Coverage  \n**Component:** All S3 operations  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- B2 rate limiting responses\n- B2 network timeout handling\n- Storage full errors\n- Authentication failures to backend\n\n**Impact:**\n- Backend error scenarios not tested\n- May not map correctly to S3 error codes\n\n**Remediation Priority:** High\n\n---\n\n#### Gap 3: Concurrent Request Handling\n\n**Type:** Test Coverage  \n**Component:** Authentication layer  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- Multiple simultaneous auth failures\n- Race conditions in signature verification\n- Concurrent access to rate-limited endpoints\n\n**Impact:**\n- Concurrency bugs may not be caught\n- Production load patterns not tested\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n\n---\n\n#### Gap 4: Header Ordering Tests\n\n**Type:** Test Coverage  \n**Component:** All endpoints  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- Tests that verify header order consistency\n- Tests for header case sensitivity\n- Tests for duplicate header detection\n\n**Impact:**\n- Header ordering may be inconsistent (noted in auth rejection docs)\n- Some clients may be sensitive to header order\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Low\n\n---\n\n#### Gap 5: Large Payload Error Handling\n\n**Type:** Test Coverage  \n**Component:** PUT operations  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- Error responses during large file uploads\n- Multipart upload abortion scenarios\n- Range request errors on large files\n\n**Impact:**\n- Large file error scenarios not verified\n- Memory issues may not be caught\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n\n---\n\n### Protocol Compliance Gaps\n\n#### Gap 6: Chunked Encoding Error Responses\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** Streaming operations  \n**Missing Implementation:**\n- Error responses during chunked upload\n- Trailer header handling on errors\n- Early termination signaling\n\n**Impact:**\n- Streaming clients may not handle errors correctly\n- May not fully support chunked encoding spec\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Low\n\n---\n\n#### Gap 7: Conditional Request Error Variations\n\n**Type:** Protocol Compliance  \n**Component:** GET/HEAD operations  \n**Missing Coverage:**\n- Multiple conditional headers combined\n- Invalid conditional header values\n- If-Range header error scenarios\n\n**Impact:**\n- Complex conditional requests not fully tested\n- Edge cases may produce unexpected errors\n\n**Remediation Priority:** Medium\n\n---\n\n## Part 3: Known Issues by Endpoint\n\n### S3-Facing Endpoints\n\n#### The plan balances AWS S3 compatibility requirements, production stability, and development effort to create a clear roadmap for improvements.\n\n### Remediation Overview\n\n| Priority Level | Issues | Total Effort | Target Timeline | Business Impact |\n|----------------|--------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|\n| **P0** | 2 | 3-5 hours | Immediate (Release 0.2.0) | Restores AWS SDK compatibility |\n| **P1** | 3 | 2-3 days | Next Sprint (Release 0.2.1) | Production readiness & correctness |\n| **P2** | 7 | 1-2 weeks | Next Quarter (Release 0.3.0) | API consistency & maintainability |\n| **P3** | 4 | 3-5 days | Future (Release 0.4.0) | Full AWS parity & edge cases |\n| **Total** | **16** | **3-4 weeks** | **Q3-Q4 2026** | **Complete compliance** |\n\n---\n\n## Priority Matrix (P0-P3)\n\n### P0: Critical - AWS SDK Compatibility (DO NOW)\n\n**Timeline:** Immediate - Must ship in Release 0.2.0  \n**Risk of Delay:** Breaking AWS SDK compatibility, untraceable requests  \n**Effort:** 3-5 hours total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **H1** | x-amz-request-id header | 2-3 hours | Low | Low | **HIGH** - Enables SDK compatibility, request tracing, support debugging |\n| **H2** | RequestId XML element | 1-2 hours | Low | Low | **HIGH** - Completes request tracing, SDK error parsing |\n\n**Dependencies:** H2 depends on H1 (request ID must be generated first)\n\n**Implementation Notes:**\n```go\n// Create new middleware: internal/server/middleware/request_id.go\npackage middleware\n\nimport (\n    \"github.com/google/uuid\"\n    \"net/http\"\n)\n\n// RequestID generates and adds x-amz-request-id header to all responses\nfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n        \n        // Store in request context for error responses\n        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), \"requestID\", requestID)\n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))\n    })\n}\n\n// Updated writeError in handlers.go\nfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    requestID := r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n</Error>`, code, message, requestID)\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify header present on all S3 responses (success & error)\n- Verify RequestId element in all error XML\n- Test request ID propagation through middleware chain\n- Validate UUID format matches AWS pattern\n\n**Quick Win:** ✅ YES - Simple middleware, immediate impact\n\n---\n\n### P1: High - Production Readiness (NEXT SPRINT)\n\n**Timeline:** Next Sprint - Target Release 0.2.1  \n**Risk of Delay:** Incorrect HTTP semantics, untested backend failures  \n**Effort:** 2-3 days total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **MH1** | x-amz-id-2 header | 1-2 hours | Low | Low | **MED** - Extended request tracing, S3 tool compatibility |\n| **MH2** | Content-Type mismatch (B2 endpoints) | 2-3 hours | Low | Low | **MED** - Correct HTTP semantics, JSON parsing |\n| **Gap 2** | Backend error propagation tests | 1-2 days | Medium | Medium | **HIGH** - Production confidence, error mapping validation |\n\n**Dependencies:** MH1 depends on P0 completion (uses request ID infrastructure)\n\n**Implementation Notes for MH1:**\n```go\n// Extend request ID middleware to include x-amz-id-2\nfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n        extendedID := generateExtendedID() // Opaque string for S3 compatibility\n        \n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-id-2\", extendedID)\n        \n        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), \"requestID\", requestID)\n        ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, \"extendedID\", extendedID)\n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))\n    })\n}\n\nfunc generateExtendedID() string {\n    // S3 uses an opaque extended ID - we can use base64 of random bytes\n    b := make([]byte, 16)\n    rand.Read(b)\n    return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for MH2:**\n```go\n// internal/server/server.go:1246-1364\n// Fix B2 endpoints to return correct Content-Type\nfunc (s *Server) handleListB2Keys(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    // ... existing logic ...\n    \n    if err != nil {\n        w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\") // FIX: was text/plain\n        w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n        json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n            \"error\": fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list keys: %v\", err),\n        })\n        return\n    }\n    // ... success case already returns application/json ...\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for Gap 2 (Backend Error Tests):**\n```go\n// internal/server/handlers/backend_test.go\nfunc TestBackendErrorPropagation(t *testing.T) {\n    tests := []struct {\n        name          string\n        simulateError func(*mockBackend)\n        expectCode    string\n        expectStatus  int\n    }{\n        {\n            name: \"B2 rate limit maps to SlowDown\",\n            simulateError: func(m *mockBackend) {\n                m.simulateRateLimit()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"SlowDown\",\n            expectStatus: 503,\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"B2 timeout maps to RequestTimeout\",\n            simulateError: func(m *mockBackend) {\n                m.simulateTimeout()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"RequestTimeout\",\n            expectStatus: 400,\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"Storage full maps to InternalError\",\n            simulateError: func(m *mockBackend) {\n                m.simulateStorageFull()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"InternalError\",\n            expectStatus: 500,\n        },\n    }\n    // ... test implementation ...\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify x-amz-id-2 format and uniqueness\n- Confirm B2 error responses return application/json\n- Run backend error simulation tests\n- Validate error code mapping matches AWS S3 behavior\n\n**Quick Wins:** ✅ YES - MH1 and MH2 are simple fixes with high value\n\n---\n\n### P2: Medium - API Consistency & Maintainability (NEXT QUARTER)\n\n**Timeline:** Next Quarter - Target Release 0.3.0  \n**Risk of Delay:** API fragmentation, maintenance burden, incomplete debugging  \n**Effort:** 1-2 weeks total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **M1** | Resource XML element | 2-3 hours | Low | Low | **MED** - Debugging context, tool compatibility |\n| **M2** | Mixed response formats (admin endpoints) | 2-3 days | Medium | Medium | **MED** - API consistency, client experience |\n| **M3** | Method Not Allowed format (405) | 1-2 days | Medium | Low | **MED** - API consistency, error handling |\n| **M4** | Duplicate writeError implementations | 3-4 hours | Low | Low | **LOW** - Code maintainability |\n| **Gap 1** | Pre-signed URL error tests | 1 day | Medium | Low | **MED** - Edge case coverage |\n| **Gap 3** | Concurrent request tests | 1 day | Medium | Medium | **MED** - Production load patterns |\n| **Gap 5** | Large payload error tests | 1 day | Medium | Low | **MED** - Memory safety, edge cases |\n| **Gap 7** | Complex conditional request tests | 1 day | Medium | Low | **LOW** - Edge case coverage |\n\n**Dependencies:** None - Can be worked in parallel\n\n**Implementation Notes for M1 (Resource element):**\n```go\n// Extract resource from request path\nfunc extractResource(r *http.Request) string {\n    // S3 resource format: /bucket/key or /bucket\n    path := r.URL.Path\n    if strings.HasPrefix(path, \"/\") {\n        return path\n    }\n    return \"/\" + path\n}\n\n// Updated writeError with Resource\nfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    requestID := r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    resource := extractResource(r)\n    \n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n  <Resource>%s</Resource>\n  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n</Error>`, code, message, resource, requestID)\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for M2 & M3 (Standardized Admin Errors):**\n```go\n// internal/pkg/errors/admin.go\npackage errors\n\nimport \"net/http\"\n\n// AdminError represents a structured admin API error\ntype AdminError struct {\n    Code           string `json:\"code\"`\n    Message        string `json:\"error\"`\n    AllowedMethods []string `json:\"allowed_methods,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// WriteAdminError writes a standardized JSON error response\nfunc WriteAdminError(w http.ResponseWriter, err AdminError, status int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n    w.WriteHeader(status)\n    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(err)\n}\n\n// Usage in handlers:\nfunc (s *Server) handleKeyRotate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    if r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n        errors.WriteAdminError(w, errors.AdminError{\n            Code:           \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n            Message:        \"Method not allowed\",\n            AllowedMethods: []string{\"POST\"},\n        }, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n        return\n    }\n    // ... existing logic ...\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for M4 (Consolidate writeError):**\n```go\n// Create: internal/pkg/errors/s3.go\npackage errors\n\nimport (\n    \"fmt\"\n    \"net/http\"\n)\n\n// WriteS3Error writes an S3-compliant XML error response\nfunc WriteS3Error(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    requestID := r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    extendedID := r.Context().Value(\"extendedID\").(string)\n    resource := extractResource(r)\n    \n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n  <Resource>%s</Resource>\n  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n  <HostId>%s</HostId>\n</Error>`, code, message, resource, requestID, extendedID)\n}\n\n// Replace all writeError calls in server.go and handlers.go\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify Resource element format matches S3 pattern\n- Test all admin endpoints return JSON errors consistently\n- Validate Method Not Allowed includes allowed methods\n- Run concurrent request tests under load\n- Test large file upload error scenarios\n- Verify pre-signed URL edge cases (expired, invalid, concurrent)\n\n**Quick Wins:** ⚠️ PARTIAL - M1, M4 are quick; M2, M3 require more effort but high value\n\n---\n\n### P3: Low - Full AWS Parity & Edge Cases (FUTURE)\n\n**Timeline:** Future Enhancement - Target Release 0.4.0  \n**Risk of Delay:** Minimal - Nice-to-have features  \n**Effort:** 3-5 days total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **L1** | CORS headers configuration | 2-3 days | High | Medium | **LOW** - AWS behavior match, security tuning |\n| **L2** | HostId XML element | 1 hour | Low | Low | **LOW** - Edge case completeness |\n| **Gap 4** | Header ordering tests | 1 day | Low | Low | **LOW** - Edge case coverage |\n| **Gap 6** | Chunked encoding error tests | 1-2 days | Medium | Low | **LOW** - Streaming edge cases |\n\n**Dependencies:** L2 depends on P0-P1 completion (uses request ID infrastructure)\n\n**Implementation Notes for L1 (Configurable CORS):**\n```go\n// Add CORS configuration to bucket config\ntype BucketCORSConfig struct {\n    AllowedOrigins     []string `json:\"allowed_origins\"`\n    AllowedMethods     []string `json:\"allowed_methods\"`\n    AllowedHeaders     []string `json:\"allowed_headers\"`\n    ExposeHeaders      []string `json:\"expose_headers\"`\n    MaxAgeSeconds      int      `json:\"max_age_seconds\"`\n    AllowCredentials  bool     `json:\"allow_credentials\"`\n}\n\n// Middleware checks bucket CORS config before adding headers\nfunc CORS(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        bucket := extractBucket(r)\n        \n        if config := getBucketCORSConfig(bucket); config != nil {\n            // Apply bucket-specific CORS rules\n            if originMatches(r.Header.Get(\"Origin\"), config.AllowedOrigins) {\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", r.Header.Get(\"Origin\"))\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", strings.Join(config.AllowedMethods, \", \"))\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", strings.Join(config.AllowedHeaders, \", \"))\n                if config.MaxAgeSeconds > 0 {\n                    w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", config.MaxAgeSeconds))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No CORS config - no headers (matches AWS behavior)\n        }\n        \n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify CORS headers respect bucket configuration\n- Test CORS preflight requests\n- Validate HostId element format\n- Run chunked encoding error scenarios\n- Test header ordering consistency\n\n**Quick Wins:** ❌ NO - Requires significant effort for low business impact\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Timeline\n\n### Phase 1: Immediate Fixes (Week 1 - Release 0.2.0)\n\n**Deliverables:**\n- ✅ x-amz-request-id header middleware\n- with_resource) as missing\nFROM armor.s3_error_responses\nWHERE http_status >= 400\n\n-- Admin endpoint format check\n| endpoint, \n  count() as total,\n  count(content_type=\"application/json\") as json_errors\nFROM armor.admin_api_errors\nGROUP BY endpoint\n```\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Updates\n\n### Documents to Update\n\n| Document | Updates Required | Priority |\n|----------|-----------------|----------|\n| `docs/error-response-headers-specification.md` | Add x-amz-request-id, x-amz-id-2 | P0 |\n| `docs/admin-endpoint-error-response-headers.md` | Update error format specification | P2 |\n| `README.md` | Update API compatibility notes | P0 |\n| API documentation | Add request ID header documentation | P0 |\n| Changelog | Document all changes per release | All |\n| `docs/bf-1jjcp7-remediation-plan.md` | Mark items complete as implemented | Ongoing |\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n### Overall Plan Acceptance\n\n- ✅ All 16 issues assigned priority levels (P0-P3)\n- ✅ Effort estimates provided for each fix (3 hours - The plan balances AWS S3 compatibility requirements, production stability, and development effort to create a clear roadmap for improvements.\n\n### Remediation Overview\n\n| Priority Level | Issues | Total Effort | Target Timeline | Business Impact |\n|----------------|--------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|\n| **P0** | 2 | 3-5 hours | Immediate (Release 0.2.0) | Restores AWS Priority Matrix (P0-P3)\n\n### P0: Critical - AWS SDK Compatibility (DO NOW)\n\n**Timeline:** Immediate - Must ship in Release 0.2.0  \n**Risk of Delay:** Breaking AWS SDK compatibility, untraceable requests  \n**Effort:** 3-5 hours total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **H1** | x-amz-request-id header | 2-3 hours | Low | Low | **HIGH** - Enables SDK compatibility, request tracing, support debugging |\n| **H2** | RequestId XML element | 1-2 hours | Low | Low | **HIGH** - Completes request tracing, SDK error parsing |\n\n**Dependencies:** H2 depends on H1 (request ID must be generated first)\n\n**Implementation Notes:**\n```go\n// Create new middleware: internal/server/middleware/request_id.go\npackage middleware\n\nimport (\n    \"github.com/google/uuid\"\n    \"net/http\"\n)\n\n// RequestID generates and adds x-amz-request-id header to all responses\nfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n        \n        // Store in request context for error responses\n        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), \"requestID\", requestID)\n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))\n    })\n}\n\n// Updated writeError in handlers.go\nfunc writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    requestID := r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n  <RequestId>%s</RequestId>\n</Error>`, code, message, requestID)\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify header present on all S3 responses (success & error)\n- Verify RequestId element in all error XML\n- Test request ID propagation through middleware chain\n- Validate UUID format matches AWS pattern\n\n**Quick Win:** ✅ YES - Simple middleware, immediate impact\n\n---\n\n### P1: High - Production Readiness (NEXT SPRINT)\n\n**Timeline:** Next Sprint - Target Release 0.2.1  \n**Risk of Delay:** Incorrect HTTP semantics, untested backend failures  \n**Effort:** 2-3 days total\n\n| Issue ID | Component | Effort | Complexity | Risk | Business Impact |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|------|-----------------|\n| **MH1** | x-amz-id-2 header | 1-2 hours | Low | Low | **MED** - Extended request tracing, S3 tool compatibility |\n| **MH2** | Content-Type mismatch (B2 endpoints) | 2-3 hours | Low | Low | **MED** - Correct HTTP semantics, JSON parsing |\n| **Gap 2** | Backend error propagation tests | 1-2 days | Medium | Medium | **HIGH** - Production confidence, error mapping validation |\n\n**Dependencies:** MH1 depends on P0 completion (uses request ID infrastructure)\n\n**Implementation Notes for MH1:**\n```go\n// Extend request ID middleware to include x-amz-id-2\nfunc RequestID(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        requestID := uuid.New().String()\n        extendedID := generateExtendedID() // Opaque string for S3 compatibility\n        \n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-request-id\", requestID)\n        w.Header().Set(\"x-amz-id-2\", extendedID)\n        \n        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), \"requestID\", requestID)\n        ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, \"extendedID\", extendedID)\n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))\n    })\n}\n\nfunc generateExtendedID() string {\n    // S3 uses an opaque extended ID - we can use base64 of random bytes\n    b := make([]byte, 16)\n    rand.Read(b)\n    return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for MH2:**\n```go\n// internal/server/server.go:1246-1364\n// Fix B2 endpoints to return correct Content-Type\nfunc (s *Server) handleListB2Keys(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    // ... existing logic ...\n    \n    if err != nil {\n        w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\") // FIX: was text/plain\n        w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n        json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n            \"error\": fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list keys: %v\", err),\n        })\n        return\n    }\n    // ... success case already returns application/json ...\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation Notes for Gap 2 (Backend Error {\n                m.simulateRateLimit()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"SlowDown\",\n            expectStatus: 503,\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"B2 timeout maps to RequestTimeout\",\n            simulateError: func(m *mockBackend) {\n                m.simulateTimeout()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"RequestTimeout\",\n            expectStatus: 400,\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"Storage full maps to InternalError\",\n            simulateError: func(m *mockBackend) {\n                m.simulateStorageFull()\n            },\n            expectCode: \"InternalError\",\n            expectStatus: 500,\n        },\n    }\n    // ... test implementation ...\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify x-amz-id-2 format and uniqueness\n- Confirm B2 error responses return application/json\n- Run backend error simulation tests\n- Validate error code mapping matches AWS S3 behavior\n\n**Quick Wins:** ✅ YES - MH1 and MH2 are simple fixes http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    requestID := r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    resource := extractResource(r)\n    \n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n `json:\"code\"`\n    Message        string `json:\"error\"`\n    AllowedMethods []string `json:\"allowed_methods,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// WriteAdminError writes a standardized JSON error response\nfunc WriteAdminError(w http.ResponseWriter, err AdminError, status int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n    w.WriteHeader(status)\n    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(err)\n}\n\n// r.Context().Value(\"requestID\").(string)\n    extendedID := r.Context().Value(\"extendedID\").(string)\n    resource := extractResource(r)\n    \n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", |\n\n**Dependencies:** L2 depends on P0-P1 completion (uses request ID infrastructure)\n\n**Implementation Notes for L1 (Configurable CORS):**\n```go\n// Add CORS configuration to bucket config\ntype BucketCORSConfig struct {\n    AllowedOrigins     []string `json:\"allowed_origins\"`\n    AllowedMethods     []string `json:\"allowed_methods\"`\n    AllowedHeaders     []string `json:\"allowed_headers\"`\n    ExposeHeaders      []string `json:\"expose_headers\"`\n    MaxAgeSeconds      int      `json:\"max_age_seconds\"`\n    AllowCredentials  bool     `json:\"allow_credentials\"`\n}\n\n// Middleware checks bucket CORS config before adding headers\nfunc CORS(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        bucket := extractBucket(r)\n        \n        if config := getBucketCORSConfig(bucket); config != nil {\n            // Apply bucket-specific CORS rules\n            if originMatches(r.Header.Get(\"Origin\"), config.AllowedOrigins) {\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", r.Header.Get(\"Origin\"))\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", strings.Join(config.AllowedMethods, \", \"))\n                w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", strings.Join(config.AllowedHeaders, \", \"))\n                if config.MaxAgeSeconds > 0 {\n                    w.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", config.MaxAgeSeconds))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No CORS config - no headers (matches AWS behavior)\n        }\n        \n        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Required:**\n- Verify CORS headers respect bucket configuration\n- Test CORS preflight requests\n- Validate HostId element format\n- Run chunked encoding error scenarios\n- Test header ordering consistency\n\n**Quick Wins:** ❌ NO - Requires significant effort for low business impact\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Timeline\n\n### Phase 1: Immediate Fixes (Week 1 - Release 0.2.0)\n\n**Deliverables:**\n- ✅ x-amz-request-id header middleware\n- with_resource) as missing\nFROM armor.s3_error_responses\nWHERE http_status >= 400\n\n-- Admin endpoint format check\n| endpoint, \n  count() as total,\n  count(content_type=\"application/json\") as json_errors\nFROM armor.admin_api_errors\nGROUP BY endpoint\n```\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Updates\n\n### Documents to Update\n\n| Document | Updates Required | Priority |\n|----------|-----------------|----------|\n| `docs/error-response-headers-specification.md` | Add x-amz-request-id, x-amz-id-2 | P0 |\n| `docs/admin-endpoint-error-response-headers.md` | Update error format specification | P2 |\n| `README.md` | Update API compatibility notes | P0 |\n| API documentation | Add request ID header documentation | P0 |\n| Changelog | Document all changes per release | All |\n| `docs/bf-1jjcp7-remediation-plan.md` | Mark items complete as implemented | Ongoing |\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n### Overall Plan Acceptance\n\n- ✅ All 16 issues assigned priority levels (P0-P3)\n- ✅ Effort estimates provided for each fix (3 hours -
